title: 2026 Update to U.S. Referral and Fulfillment by Amazon Fees
summary: 2026 FBA fulfillment fees rise avg $0.08/unit effective Jan 15 2026; referral fees and fee types unchanged. Tiered by size/price band. Recompute via Revenue Calculator / Fee & Economics Preview / Profit Analytics.

title: How to Report an IP Violation to Amazon (Brand Registry / Report a Violation)
summary: Amazon enforces copyright, trademark, and patent IP only; reporting via Public Notice Form or Brand-Registry Report a Violation (needs registered trademark). 99% of 2024 suspected infringements blocked proactively. Screen brand/IP risk before buying.

title: Amazon FBA Accounting & Bookkeeping 2026 (Eightx)
summary: Decompose bi-weekly settlements on accrual basis; keep Amazon fees below gross margin not in COGS; use clearing + deposits-in-transit accounts; returns are net-zero COGS. True per-unit profit nets all fees+refunds, not the Seller Central headline.

title: Mastering Chunking Strategies for RAG (Databricks)
summary: Baseline fixed-size+overlap, then compare recursive (hierarchy-of-separators) and semantic chunking; smaller chunks often retrieve better and cheaper; attach metadata to each chunk and filter on it in search.

title: Searching for Best Practices in Retrieval-Augmented Generation (arXiv 2407.01219)
summary: RAG is a multi-step chain; empirically balances quality vs latency/efficiency. Advanced variants add complexity/latency. Multimodal retrieval-as-generation helps QA over images. Benchmark each step before adopting.

title: RAG: A Comprehensive Survey of Architectures, Enhancements, and Robustness Frontiers (arXiv 2506.00054)
summary: Taxonomy of retriever/generator/hybrid/robustness-oriented RAG. Failure modes: retrieval quality, grounding fidelity, efficiency, robustness. Trade-offs precision vs flexibility, efficiency vs faithfulness; prioritize retrieval-aware eval + grounding/citation checks.

title: Amazon Restricted Products in 2026: Categories, Requirements & How to Get Approved (Seller Labs)
summary: Screen gating before sourcing. High-gate 2026 categories: Beauty, Supplements, Health, Grocery, Electronics, Jewelry, Medical Devices, children's Textiles. Docs are category-specific (ISO/IEC 17025 CoA, FCC/UL, FDA reg + shelf life, 510(k)); plain invoice often insufficient. Ungating ~1wk-1mo, $500-$2000+. Listing un-approved risks suspension/ban. Verify each gate in Seller Central.

title: Keepa Product Finder: Every Filter Explained (2026 Guide) (FBA Mogul)
summary: ~120 filters, AND logic across sections (don't over-constrain). Always use Sales Rank; prefer 90-day avg BSR over current. 'Out of stock' checkbox means no Buy Box winner (BB filter) or Amazon's offer absent (Amazon filter), not product unavailable. OA play: filter Amazon OOS, confirm persistence with 90-day OOS% + Amazon OOS Count. Set min price ~$8-10 to clear FBA fees, max to budget; use drop% for clearance/demand.

title: Amazon FBA Fees 2026: Full Breakdown + April 17 Surcharge (AMZ Prep)
summary: Confirms ~3.5% fuel/inflation surcharge started April 17 2026 on all FBA fulfillment fees (US+CA), separate from the ~$0.08/unit Jan-15 rise ($5.00 fee -> ~$5.18). Aged-inventory surcharges start ~90 days earlier (~181 days). Inbound defect fees jumped to ~$0.32-$5.72/unit. Amazon ended its FBA prep services Jan 1 2026; new Overmax surcharge for extra-large; West-Coast storage rising. Verify per-ASIN officially.

title: Online-Optimized RAG for Tool Use and Function Calling (arXiv 2509.20415)
summary: Deployment-time framework that adapts retrieval embeddings from live interactions using minimal feedback (task success/failure), via lightweight online gradient updates with negligible latency and no LLM change. Fixes embedding misalignment causing wrong-tool retrieval. Supports single/multi-hop tool use, dynamic tool inventories, top-K + re-rank. Gains depend on decent embedding init; benchmarked on tool-use/doc data, not FBA. Maps to learning scout/RAG from realized outcomes.

title: Amazon Online Arbitrage Product Sourcing 101 (2026 Beginners Guide)
summary: Beginner OA sourcing walkthrough. Winning-product criteria: <200k BSR, 30+ sales/mo, $3 profit, 30%+ ROI. SellerAmp+Keepa+Boxem ~$99/mo stack; storefront stalking as the primary method; Boxem bulk auto-ungate checking while stalking; read Keepa offer-count trend (stable/falling good) + Buy-Box price; manufacture margin via coupons/cashback/discounted gift cards/tax-exempt. 'You make items good, you don't find them good.'

title: The Online Arbitrage Strategy That's Creating Sustainable Cash Flow in 2026
summary: Cash-flow strategy: keep a persistent lead bank and allocate weekly capital to the best saved leads instead of chasing daily clearance; buy price-cycle lows and sell the 2-8 week upswing; prefer retail-priced evergreen reverse-sourced listings over clearance (which saturates on FBA); re-buy sold-through winners first; scale with a VA (~$400-600/mo) near $20k revenue/mo.

title: How To Use Keepa For Online Arbitrage in 2026
summary: Keepa signals for OA: offer-count volatility (sellers cycling on/off) flags real arbitrage vs PL/wholesale; source during offer-count downtrends when price peaks and margins widen; sharp cliff drops in offers indicate IP-complaint/delisting risk (avoid); use the Offers tab to reverse-source from sellers who entered at peak-price windows, verifying buy-box rotation and seller tenure.

title: Finding Winning Products To Resell on Amazon FBA (Reverse Sourcing Masterclass 2026)
summary: Reverse-sourcing framework: store-brand names and same-review-count sellers as verified entry points; a five-gate rapid disqualifier (Buy-Box suppression >=6mo, Amazon on listing, high offers-to-sales ratio, >20% price volatility, all-large-seller competition) cuts sourcing to under ~20s per listing; confirm buy-box rotation <50%/seller, ~200+ sales/mo, FBA/FBM mix, and 6-month price stability before a test order; coupon-stack when within ~5% of the ROI target.

title: NEW Storefront Stalking Reveal
summary: A '3-tool' storefront-stalking workflow that is essentially the standard method repackaged: SellerAmp brand filters (>=~200 products, <=~6 sellers/listing, <=~1% Amazon) -> find profitable sellers -> Storefront Stalker to scan their catalog; plus a reverse path (strong ASIN -> extract seller ID -> filter their storefront by BSR <~180k + weight). Most useful bit: check the Keepa break-even price first and skip if retail cost is above it.

title: How To Start Amazon Online Arbitrage In 2026 (Full Beginners Guide)
summary: Fresh 2026 beginner OA guide: storefront stalking is the primary research method (replicate proven sellers, not random clearance); ungating focuses on 1-2 gated brands plus an auto-ungate list via Boxem's bulk checker (expect 10-20 rejections per gated brand); profit relies on coupons + discounted gift cards + subscribe-and-save + resale/tax-exempt certs, not baseline retail; tool stack ~$99/mo (SellerAmp ~$20 + Keepa ~$29 + Boxem ~$50), both offer 2-week trials.

title: SellerAmp SAS Masterguide for 2026
summary: Configure Offer Summary + Buy Box Analysis panels before trusting numbers. Estimated-Sales '!' means the figure is shared across variations, not per-variation. Alerts (e.g. suspected private label) mean investigate deeper, not auto-skip; color-coded green/amber/red/blue. BSR is category-relative - check category size via Ranks & Prices before judging a rank. Max Cost can be worth exceeding on cheap items that double your money. Use Profit Calculator cost field to model coupon/tax/multi-buy stacking. Multi-year charts reveal seasonal demand spikes.

title: Listings Restrictions API (Amazon SP-API)
summary: getListingsRestrictions (SP-API v2021-08-01) programmatically checks listing restrictions by ASIN or brand+product-type across multiple marketplaces in one call, with optional condition-type filtering; returns approval next-step links when restricted; evaluates GTIN-exemption auto-approval when brand+product-type given. Sellers-only, requires Product Listing role. Pairs with Listings Items API (check then create). Concrete API path for automating fba-compliance-checker eligibility checks if ever integrated - not currently wired up.

title: Amazon cash flow management: the FBA seller playbook (Nova Analytics)
summary: DD+7 disbursements since March 12 2026 (delivery-confirmed + 7 days hold + 3-5 day bank transfer; fast Prime FBA pays faster than the old 14-day cycle, slow FBM stretches to 17-21 days). Reserve = 3-12% of recent revenue (50-100% first 90 days), grows with velocity - model as % of trailing 14-day sales. Five cash leaks: reserve growth on hot months, refunds posting to the next settlement, Oct-Jan storage spikes, PPC as cash outflow, COGS-to-cash lag (~70 days, underestimated ~30%). CCC = DIO+DSO-DPO, typical FBA 60-120 days. Weekly Monday routine: disbursement vs forecast, reserve WoW, days-of-inventory by category, rolling 28-day contribution margin per SKU; investigate >10% moves.

title: 11 Keepa Product Finder Hacks for Profitable Amazon Sourcing (Tall Oak Advisors)
summary: Bulk-brand filter via Excel list separated by ### pasted into the brand filter; Buy Box 90-days-drop% in a tight -20..20 band to screen for price stability; negative keywords (-term) to prune niches; storefront stalking by searching a competitor's favorite ASINs/brands in PF; dropping the sales-rank filter to find rankless items with steady price history + growing reviews; Chris Grant's Corridor method (tight price band + strict demand filters, re-run weekly); wholesale leads via consistent Buy-Box-owner filtering; URL-decoding saved PF bookmarks to learn advanced query construction; Amazon-to-Amazon flips on dip/spike cycles.

title: Amazon Sellers Are Falling Behind: The New Rules of Sourcing Products in 2026 (Boxem)
summary: Four 2026 sourcing shifts: (1) work the Amazon search page - unprofitable single-packs often have profitable multi-pack/bundle variants with their own Buy Box (SellerAmp Quick View Simplified puts rank/FBA-count/ASIN on the results page); (2) AI-assisted discount-path finding (signup coupons + discounted gift cards turn a pass into ~48% ROI); (3) bulk ungate checking while storefront stalking - only chase cleared ASINs, beginners clear 50-100 brands on a first auto-ungate scan; (4) retail price is a lie - margin is created by stacking coupons/gift cards/rewards, reseller certificate (5-10%), tax-free prep center, FBM for <1lb items. Vendor blog (Boxem) - discount tool pitches.

title: Amazon Meltable FBA Inventory: All You Need to Know (Seller Assistant)
summary: Meltables (chocolate, gummies/gel supplements, wax melts, some cosmetics; anything degrading at 155F) are FBA-inboundable only Oct 16 - Apr 14; during Apr 15 - Oct 15 they arrive unfulfillable and may be disposed of at seller cost, with listing suppression on complaints. Remove unsold meltables before ~early April (removals take 10-14 business days). Amazon publishes a downloadable Meltable ASIN List. Reclassification appeal needs a manufacturer letter proving 155F tolerance. FBM still allowed in the restricted window with insulated packaging + gel packs + 1-2-day shipping. Practitioner restating Amazon policy; official help page G200140860 is JS-rendered/unfetchable - verify dates in Seller Central.

title: Dashboard Design Patterns for Modern Web Apps in 2026 (Art of Styleframe)
summary: Operator-dashboard specs from Linear/Stripe/Grafana patterns: 4-6 KPI cards above the fold (one 28-32px number + one comparison + ONE visual; >5-7 primary metrics degrades decisions); sidebar 256px expanded / 64px icon rail, 36px items; 12-col CSS Grid with 24px gutters + container queries; three mandatory states per component - content-shaped skeletons (not spinners), honest empty states (illustration + one sentence + CTA), component-scoped error banners with retry (never page-blocking modals); tables: sticky solid header, 36-40px dense rows, right-aligned numbers, pagination for reference data; override chart-library default palettes (fail WCAG contrast). Confirms the control-center's data-dense, honest-states design system.

title: Retrieval Augmented Generation Evaluation in the Era of Large Language Models: A Comprehensive Survey (arXiv 2504.14891)
summary: The most comprehensive RAG-evaluation survey (18pp): systematically reviews traditional + LLM-driven evaluation for system performance, factual accuracy, safety, and efficiency; separates retrieval-component metrics from generation/grounding metrics; compiles RAG-specific datasets and automated frameworks (ARES-style) with a meta-analysis of evaluation practice in high-impact RAG research. Reference catalog for building the missing eval harness over Ask - matches the guardrails' retrieval-aware-eval + citation-check split.

title: Uncertainty Quantification for Retrieval-Augmented Reasoning (arXiv 2510.11483)
summary: R2C: estimate confidence for multi-step retrieval-augmented reasoning by perturbing reasoning steps - perturbations alter the retriever's input, which shifts retrieved context and the generator's next-step input, capturing uncertainty from both retriever and generator. >5% average AUROC improvement over SOTA UQ baselines across five RAR systems. Design input for Ask calibration: flag low-confidence answers by re-querying with perturbed retrieval instead of sounding equally confident on weakly-grounded answers. Not implemented - staged as design input.

title: How to Get Ungated on Amazon in 2026 | Free Ungating Guide
summary: Three approval tiers: auto-ungated, gated-not-restricted (invoice application), restricted. Boxem's bulk-ungate suggested-ASINs scanner auto-applies to 50-100 brands free in ~10 minutes. Invoice mechanics: buy from the brand's own website or a major retailer (never a random wholesaler); submit the full order-ship-delivery confirmation as one PDF; billing/shipping address must match the seller account; buy ~10 units regardless of the quantity Amazon's form implies it wants - multiple submission attempts are normal. On rejection: wait 24h, add supporting evidence; after repeated declines, physically printing and photographing the same real documents can route review to a human. Paid ungating services ($200-$5,000+) do nothing but this same trick.

title: The ULTIMATE SellerAmp SAS for Amazon FBA Tutorial (Step by Step for Beginners)
summary: Full SellerAmp SAS feature walkthrough. Estimated monthly sales is a floor (built from Keepa BSR drops), not a count - sales occur while BSR rises too, just slower. Buy-Box price-change counts split increases from decreases - a high count of both signals competitive jockeying, not a genuine decline. The Buy-Box Analysis 'all sellers ever, sorted by last-seen' view is an underused storefront-stalking tool - sellers off this ASIN for months are still stalkable elsewhere; skip 5,000+-review single-category sellers (usually wholesale). Hovering an offer shows a seller's ASIN count + category spread as a fast OA-vs-wholesale heuristic. Multiple SellerAmp profiles let a stricter lead-gen threshold (e.g. $4 min profit/30% min ROI) coexist with a looser personal profile. Advanced Search 'safety net' recipe: band Buy-Box-with-shipping price so current/30-day-avg/90-day-avg ALL agree (not just one 90-day snapshot) plus a bounded 30-day drop% and min offer count - a stronger stability signal than a single timeframe.

title: Why Amazon FBA Sourcing Will Be Harder in 2026 (And How Modern Arbitrage Resellers Are Preparing Now)
summary: Manual sourcing now yields 2-3 testable products/session vs 10, due to competition + GS1/GTIN/brand-gating enforcement (e.g. Oct-2025 Trader Joe's GTIN wave). Three-punch workflow: Keepa storefront-stalk up to 50 storefront IDs at once to extract all active ASINs; ArbiSource reverse-scan across 240+ retail stores; custom-scan any other store. Key habit: save every ASIN including non-buys - leads become viable in 2-6 months as price/stock/competition/gating change; rescan your own lead bank ('shopping from the back of the list') instead of starting from zero. Validates the leads-table + outcomes design; vendor 25X/1-6th-cost math is a pitch, the mechanism is the takeaway.

title: Bookkeeping for Amazon Sellers: The Complete Guide (Beancount.io)
summary: Chart of accounts for Amazon sellers: one expense account per fee type (referral, FBA fulfillment, storage, ads, subscription, returns processing), all OUTSIDE COGS; COGS = product cost + duties + freight-in + prep only. Refunds are contra-revenue; lost/damaged reimbursements are taxable income (missing them creates 1099-K mismatches). Reconcile every biweekly settlement against gross sales, not the net deposit. FBA inventory placement can create sales-tax nexus in other states (matters if selling off-Amazon; marketplace-facilitator laws cover Amazon-side). US quarterly estimated taxes 2026: Apr 15 / Jun 16 / Sep 15 / Jan 15 2027; safe harbor 100% of prior year (110% if AGI >$150k). Outcomes/realized-ROI fields should mirror this fee-type split.

title: Amazon Fee Changes 2026: Significant Fee Updates (Seller Snap)
summary: Consolidated 2026 fee map (effective Jan 15 2026, updated Apr 2026), each section linking the official Seller Central rate card: low-inventory-level fee now seller-FNSKU-level, triggers only when both 30-day AND 90-day days-of-supply <28 ($0.32-$2.09/unit; new sellers exempt 365 days; <20 units/7 days exempt); returns-processing fee only above category return-rate thresholds (Grocery 2.9% to Computers 11.4%; apparel per-unit no threshold; <25 units/mo exempt); non-SIPP bulky packaging fee ~$2.07/unit; Overmax surcharges $17-$25/unit for >96-inch items; inbound defect fee consolidated ~$0.60/unit; Low-Price FBA discount $0.86 vs $10+ items; removal 0-0.5 lb down to $0.84; liquidation unchanged (15% + processing fee); Large Bulky split into Small/Large Bulky; dimensional-weight basis for large tiers.

title: Enhancing Retrieval-Augmented Generation: A Study of Best Practices (arXiv 2501.07391)
summary: Systematic ablation of RAG components: query expansion, retrieval strategy variants, Contrastive In-Context Learning KB, LM size, prompt design, document chunk size, knowledge-base size, retrieval stride, multilingual KBs, and sentence-level Focus Mode retrieval. Directly relevant to knowledge-rag's tunable knobs (chunking, KB growth, retrieval params). Abstract staged only - read the PDF before changing chunking/retrieval, and pair with the staged RAG-eval survey (2504.14891) so knob changes are measured. Code public.

title: The Keepa Parameter Method! Advanced Keepa Data Product Finder Sourcing Tutorial & Live OA Sourcing!
summary: Reverse-engineers a known-good OA lead's Keepa graph into Keepa Product Finder filters: sets 30/60/90/180-day average sales-rank and buy-box-price ranges (not the noisy 'current' values) around a winning listing's own history, plus category, Amazon-out-of-stock, minimum offer count (3+), and no-variations, to surface similarly-shaped ASINs. Judgment heuristics while reviewing results: offer count decreasing = buyable signal, rising = pass signal; verify big-box 'sold by' listings are genuinely first-party. Cites ~40% ROI / ~$4 profit as an acceptable target and notes peak-Q4 fee rates cost ~$1/unit versus non-Q4.

title: How To Use KEEPA For Amazon FBA In 2026 || Beginner To Expert In ONE Video
summary: Comprehensive Keepa-reading walkthrough: read BSR against the parent category (not subcategory); a flat offer-count line even at a high number means a listing isn't moving; a rapid seller-count jump precedes a price crash; never price off current spot alone, always check 1+ year history and seasonality. Amazon-on-listing only disqualifies if Amazon holds the Buy Box almost exclusively (check Buy Box Statistics splits); sharp seller-count collapses ('Keepa cliffs') are the strongest signal of active brand IP enforcement, stronger than the supplementary ipalert.com lookup. Avoid misbranded/generic listings (Section 3 suspension risk) and single-seller optimized private-label listings. Evaluate variation children individually; for sparse-data listings triangulate via rating trend + Offers-tab sold counts + stock-level changes; treat Keepa as a high-throughput screening tool, test uncertain leads with small quantities.

title: Amazon Online Arbitrage: How To Succeed in 2026 (Flips4Miles full walkthrough)
summary: Full OA course: sourcing methods (storefront stalking, leaf sourcing, seasonal Buy-Box-history sourcing), Keepa/SellerAmp reading rules (parent-listing BSR vs variations, offer-count/price patterns, IP-cliff signature, Amazon Buy Box share thresholds), margin-manufacturing via discount/cashback/tax-exemption stacking, FBA vs FBM profitability crossover by weight/price, buy criteria (BSR/ROI/profit by category), ungating via 10+-unit invoice documentation, a detailed 2026 seasonal calendar (Nov 15 FBA cutoff, Dec 5-20 restock window), and scaling notes (test-order sizing, prep centers, VAs, repricers, niche tradeoffs). Notes retail dropshipping as a policy violation.

title: The Complete Online Arbitrage Guide for 2026 (Ecom Circles)
summary: 2026 OA reality: 10-20% realistic net margins; 40% gross-margin floor incl. full COGS (inbound, 3PL prep $0.50-2/unit, 2-5% inspection loss); asserted 2026 rules to verify: commingling ends Mar 31 (manufacturer barcodes), Amazon FBA prep ended Jan 1, retail receipts no longer ungate (wholesale invoice/LOA only); test 20-50 units, scale at 10+ units/week + BSR<30K; phased scaling roadmap $1K to $30K+/mo.

title: Reverse Sourcing for Amazon Sellers: The 5-Step Workflow (2026 Guide) (B2B Supplier Hub)
summary: Reverse sourcing's top use = 2nd/3rd authorized suppliers for existing winners, not new products. 2026 chain-of-custody enforcement: supplier must PROVE authorization, middleman stock can fail verification. 5 steps: proven product (UPC/brand/MPN) -> map distributor network -> confirm authorization + compliant invoice before paying -> compare cost/MOQ/lead/terms -> keep 2-3-supplier bench per top SKU. Sports ~3-5 distributors/SKU vs electronics ~20-25/brand.

title: Everything on Storefront Stalking as an Arbitrage Seller (Stealth Seller)
summary: Storefront-quality screen: mixed-category, <~1,000 products, irregular smaller listing batches (small researching reseller), no Amazon on their listings; avoid huge single-brand storefronts (wholesale). Confirms staged stalking method; usable as a storefront-scoring filter.

title: Current List of Amazon Gated & Restricted Brands (The Selling Guys, updated Jul 1 2026)
summary: 750+ brand tracker tiered by risk (legal action > C&D > IP warning > gated > low-risk). Gated: Nike, Lego (US), Fitbit, Under Armour, Logitech. Legal/C&D: Apple, Bose, Ninja, NutriBullet, Razer, Makita, Netgear. Rules: country-specific gating but action anywhere predicts action everywhere; don't stock risky brands; check per-ASIN on your account. Candidate ai-brain avoid-list input via fba-brain-updater review.

title: Xero Bookkeeping for Amazon FBA 2026: Settlement Reconciliation Guide (Eightx)
summary: Xero implementation of 'never book the net deposit': $47K deposit = $68K gross - $21K fees; one account per fee type, fees never in COGS. A2X ($69/mo) >$50K/mo, Link My Books <$50K/mo; reconcile within 5 business days per settlement; reserves = Amazon Receivables asset; weighted-average landed-cost COGS. Case: '60%' gross margin was really 52% unbundled; Amazon CM2 28% vs DTC 41% -> budget reallocation.

title: Scaling RAG with RAG Fusion: Lessons from an Industry Deployment (arXiv 2603.02153, Dell)
summary: Production evidence: multi-query retrieval + RRF raised raw recall but gains neutralized after reranking + truncation; fusion did NOT beat single-query baselines (Hit@10 0.51 -> 0.48 in several configs) and added latency. For knowledge-rag: with a reranker, single-query + rerank may dominate; A/B before adopting fusion. Staged from abstract only (PDF unreadable this run).

title: A Hybrid Retrieval and Reranking Framework for Evidence-Grounded RAG (arXiv 2605.01664)
summary: Citation-aware RAG (Bedrock KB, Titan V2, OpenSearch, hybrid retrieval -> Cohere rerank) with an auditable grounding harness: separate LLM judge, claim-level binary support decisions, no outside knowledge, vague/partial evidence = unsupported, all stages persisted as CSVs. 100% grounding on only 25 queries - steal the evaluation design (matches Ask's honest-citation guardrail), not the number.

title: How to Read the SellerAmp SAS Charts Panel (Keepa Data)
summary: Official SellerAmp doc (Jun 2026): Charts panel = licensed Keepa data in every lookup - historic BSR, Amazon/Buy Box/lowest-FBA/FBM prices, offer+review counts over 30/90/180d/full. Read as a pair: rank history sanity-checks monthly-sales estimates; price history reveals spike vs normal price. Frequent BSR drops = regular sales; stable price = less race-to-bottom risk.

title: Arbitrage on Amazon: The 2026 Playbook for Finding & Selling (EntreResource)
summary: System-not-hustle playbook: strict buy order (eligibility -> red flags -> fees/history/seller count -> quantity LAST); always subtract a return allowance (most-skipped line, makes weak buys look profitable); sellable margin not spreadsheet margin; BSR is a filter not a decision; current Buy Box is not a reliable future price; 30% ROI + $5/unit floor; suspension triggers = restricted brands, inauthenticity, IP complaints.

title: Online Arbitrage: Getting Started for New Amazon Sellers in 2026 (Aura)
summary: 2026 state-of-OA (repricer vendor, discount pitch claims): market rewards systematic operators; 5 sourcing strategies (manual, automated scans, reverse sourcing, VA, lead lists - combine 2-3); floors $3/30%/BSR<100k, realized 10-15%/item; 3-month inventory rule, 30/60/90-day removal cadence, <=20% capital per ASIN, IPI>450; storage fee math incl. long-term $6.90/cu ft; margin stacking (card+portal+gift cards+coupons+loyalty) lifted example 40%->68% ROI.

title: RAGVA: Engineering RAG-based Virtual Assistants in Practice (arXiv 2502.14930)
summary: Transurban+Monash experience report: 8 engineering challenges for production RAG assistants (data/scope, security guardrails, LLM-churn maintenance, relevancy-vs-conciseness, automated testing incl. missing test oracle, systematic eval metrics, human-feedback loop at scale, Responsible AI) + 22 RQs. Core: RAG apps are nondeterministic -> continuous validation replaces spec-based test plans. Maps ~1:1 to knowledge-rag/Ask risk register.

title: FLAIR: Feedback Learning for Adaptive Information Retrieval (arXiv 2508.13390)
summary: Microsoft, deployed in Copilot DECO: lightweight feedback-learning for retrieval. Offline: indicators from user feedback + questions synthesized from docs (cheap bootstrap); online: two-track ranking blends raw similarity with indicators - augments, not replaces, vector search so similarity stays inspectable. Gains on seen and unseen queries at production scale. Candidate match_chunks upgrade once Ask collects thumbs feedback; synthetic-questions usable now.

title: How to Prepare Inventory for Amazon FBA in 2026 (Snapl)
summary: 3PL guide to 2026 FBA prep: Amazon ended US prep/labeling services Jan 1 2026 and FCs no longer fix errors at check-in - seller owns prep fully. Unit rules (one scannable barcode, cover competing barcodes, polybag suffocation warnings, visible expiry dates), bundles as one unit/one barcode, carton-label placement failures, and a unit/carton/process QC checklist. Flags unverified Mar-31-2026 FNSKU-mandate claim for non-Brand-Registry resellers - verify vs Seller Central G200141500.

title: Amazon seller tax: A guide for 2026 (QuickBooks)
summary: 2026 tax year: 1099-K threshold back to $20k AND 200 transactions (all income reportable regardless); marketplace-facilitator laws mean Amazon collects/remits sales tax nearly everywhere BUT FBA placement creates physical nexus in ~44 states, economic nexus ~$100k triggers registration, some states want zero-dollar returns; product tax codes prevent over-collection; resale certificates avoid sales tax on inventory buys; Schedule C + deductions (software/tools, shipping, home office).

title: IP Complaint Amazon: How to Prevent Listing Takedowns (SellerApp)
summary: IP complaints suppress the ASIN immediately with no investigation; check Account Health > Policy Compliance daily and treat silently-inactive listings as possible IP flags. Filers include agencies/aggregators/rogue competitors, not just brands. Zero-tolerance brands: Nike, Apple, Disney, LEGO, OtterBox, Funko, Beats, Hasbro. Playbook: valid -> remove ASIN, request retraction, POA, keep docs 180d; invalid -> evidence then notice-dispute@amazon.com; DMCA counter-notice copyright-only, starts 10-14 day legal clock. Keep an own IP-complaint tracker.

title: ProductResearch: Training E-Commerce Deep Research Agents (arXiv 2602.23716)
summary: Alibaba: deep-research agents don't transfer to e-commerce without fusing open-web evidence with structured catalog queries. Multi-agent synthesis - User Agent (persona+query+per-query rubric), Research Agent (Plan->Toolcall->Report over web+catalog tools), Supervisor Agent (3-state machine verifying every step, targeted feedback) - distilled into single-role SFT data. Qwen3-30B-A3B: RACE 31.78->45.40, product coverage 3.58->12.45. Liftable: step-level supervisor verification for scout/Ask; rubric-per-query eval.

title: Keepa product finder tutorial 2026 | Amazon FBA
summary: Live Keepa Product Finder filter waterfall (top 1-2% BSR per SellerAmp's sales-rank table -> offer count 3-15, since <3 is almost always private label and >15 is oversaturated -> buy-box not Amazon -> rating >=4 -> package weight cap) drills millions of candidates to a workable few hundred. IP-risk chart reading: a sudden flatline offer-count drop (20->4 sellers in a day) signals a real IP complaint; a gradual staircase decline is normal sell-through. Price during an Amazon-out-of-stock spike is not sustainable -- size buy cost off the lowest 3-month price. Storefront-stalk sellers with ~50-1,000 reviews (5,000+ is usually wholesale). Cash back explicitly not counted as reliable margin.

title: How to Use Amazon FBA Profit Calculator | Every Fee Every Cost Explained 2026
summary: Vendor tool demo (AMZ Prep) -- verify claims against Amazon's own fee pages. Claims Amazon introduced 2026 price tiers (<$10 / $10-50 / >$50) each with a different FBA fulfillment rate, and bills on dimensional weight when higher than actual weight. Worked example: Health category referral fee 15%; aged-inventory fee $0 at ~2mo turnover. Tool's 'healthy' bar is 20%+ MARGIN, a different metric from our 30% ROI gate.

title: Storefront Stalking is NOT a Profitable Sourcing Method | Amazon FBA Sourcing Tutorial
summary: Short rebuttal video. Useful tip: Keepa Product Finder beats SellerAmp for storefront stalking because you can sort a seller's storefront directly by units-sold-in-past-month, prioritizing fast movers instead of paging through SellerAmp's slower interface. Also promotes a paid third-party SaaS ('Arbitrage Stalker') for automated storefront-change alerts -- market awareness only.

title: Live Online Arbitrage Sourcing: Storefront Stalking Tutorial | Amazon FBA Sourcing
summary: Live demo proving storefront-stalking quality is entirely seed-dependent: starting from a weak generic seed brand (Great Value) wasted the session on dead-end storefronts (low-volume/dropshipping/wholesale sellers). Starting from an established brand (Adidas Originals) filtered on 'Amazon out of stock >=95% of the time + listed >=1yr' found a profitable variation within minutes. Relevant to discovery_hints.py's brand-seed selection: seed quality matters more than technique.

title: Beginners Guide To Amazon FBA Online Arbitrage in 2026 (Free Course)
summary: Full OA fundamentals course. Core signal repeated heavily: offer count falling -> price rises; offer count rising -> price falls, avoid. Buy-Box suppression in the last 6-8mo = reduce order size as a hedge. One seller with disproportionate buy-box % at a shared price point = inventory-advantage risk (mainly wholesale competition). Ungating claim (unverified against Amazon docs): invoices matter less now than account health + total sales, ~200 sales cited as an auto-approval threshold. Never fold cash back into buy-cost math; stack gift-card sites with coupons. Buy wide not deep, reorder ~3-4wk of stock by observed sell-through. States a personal 30% min ROI -- matches ai-brain.json's minRoi=0.3 exactly.

title: Hybrid Search for RAG: Vector + Keyword + Reranking Guide 2026 (BuildMVPFast)
summary: Postgres-native RAG retrieval upgrade path for knowledge-rag: pure vector search misses exact-match tokens (error codes, SKUs, acronyms, ~35% of some query sets); fix is hybrid = vector + BM25 merged with Reciprocal Rank Fusion (RRF, k=60), then cross-encoder rerank of top 20-50 down to top 5-10. Weaviate/BEIR: hybrid+rerank lifts Success@1 0.43->0.52, Recall@5 0.70->0.81, nDCG@10 0.61->0.70. Fits Supabase stack via pgvector (dense) + ParadeDB pg_search (BM25). Gotchas: normalize scores before weighted blending, enrich uniform chunks with section titles for BM25, adjust weights by query intent, rerank 20-50 (not 5/200). ~200-400ms added latency. Staged, not implemented.

title: 5 Keepa Power Moves + 60-SOP tactic taxonomy (OA Challenge)
summary: Named taxonomy of Keepa Product Finder sourcing tactics (procedures behind Scribehow embeds/paid playbook, so map-not-manual). 5 headline plays: batch-stalk multiple storefronts in one KPF search; find profitable FBM inventory; filter for stable-priced ASINs; find brand-new (<1mo) products before saturation; reverse-source by brand. Advanced tactics worth knowing by name for scout discovery-hint ideation: lead synthesizer (1 lead -> many), A2A flips, KPF negative keywords, buy-the-pinch/OOS sourcing, filtering ASINs with no current sales rank via BSR history + review-count growth, estimating monthly sales from review count, cross-border via Compare International Prices.

title: Keepa Charts: The Ultimate Amazon FBA Tutorial for 2026
summary: Precise Keepa chart walkthrough. Monthly-sold gold line is a customer-count RANGE (50+/100+/200+), not literal units, trailing 30 days, only ~3.5M of 1B+ ASINs get it; losing the line doesn't mean sales stopped. Sales-rank drops/month has no fixed correlation to units sold. Buy Box price is regionally directional (Amazon's flywheel model), not exact. Suppressed buy box slows sales, doesn't stop them. Seasonal rule: be first in or last out. Buy framework: sales-rank + buy-box trend + Amazon history + FBA-vs-FBM price gap ('FBA bump') + offer-count + rating-count; frame as an asymmetric bet (worst historical low still ~breaks even).

title: Sourcing Overlooked Listings Using Keepa Product Finder
summary: Grocery bundle/multi-pack and no-UPC listings are systematically missed by barcode-scanning RA and UPC-matching tools since a multi-pack's UPC doesn't match single-unit product data. Product Finder recipe: category=Grocery, size=pack (select all), 30-day sales-rank cutoff, buy-box price band, Amazon-out-of-stock toggle. Separate technique: filter a broad category pull to rows where the UPC column is blank. Always cross-check the listing's stated brand actually matches current sellers.

title: Amazon Online Arbitrage Product Sourcing MASTERCLASS For 2026
summary: Full beginner course, mostly overlapping earlier ingested videos. New items: Boxom's bulk ungate checker (checks up to 5 brands/sec for auto-ungate eligibility, vs one-by-one manual checks). When there's a gap between FBM buy box and lowest FBA offer, price FBA 5-10% above the FBM buy box (Amazon's delivery-speed preference still wins share at a premium). Restates manufacturing-margin stacking (coupon+tax-exempt+cashback+subscribe&save+discounted gift cards, naming CardBear alongside CardCookie/Raise). COVID-era seasonal pool-price example ($147->$325->$475) illustrating that arbitrage is mostly about boring reliably-profitable products, not spikes.
